title: Transnational Crime
description: What are the major international organized crime groups, their structures, activities, and global impact?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive information on organized crime entities that operate across national borders, including drug cartels, human‑trafficking syndicates, arms smuggling networks, and other illicit groups. Relevant documents should describe specific transnational criminal organizations, their hierarchical or networked structures, the types of illegal activities they conduct, the regions they operate in, and recent law‑enforcement investigations or actions against them.

title: Polio Outbreaks

narrative: The user seeks current reports of polio cases, including details such as location, date, and severity, as well as any discussion of post‑polio syndrome or related sequelae. Relevant documents describe recent outbreaks (e.g., the reappearance of poliomyelitis in Peru) or address post‑polio health issues. Documents that only cover unrelated diseases, historical outbreaks far before the query period, or lack any mention of polio or post‑polio effects are not relevant.

title: Hubble Achievements
description: What major scientific achievements has the Hubble Space Telescope accomplished since its launch?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive list of Hubble’s notable accomplishments from its 1990 launch to the present, including specific discoveries, observations, and contributions to astronomy and cosmology. Relevant documents should enumerate individual achievements (e.g., gravitational lens studies, precise age-of‑universe measurements, imaging the most distant galaxies, insights into dark matter distribution, etc.), provide dates or timeframes when available, and explain the significance of each achievement.

description: Which mammal species are classified as endangered and what are their natural habitats?
narrative: The user wants a detailed list of mammal species that are officially listed as endangered, accompanied by concise information about the habitats or geographic regions each species occupies. Relevant results should name specific endangered mammals and describe the ecosystems they depend on; broader conservation policy discussions are only tangentially relevant.

Documents that rank or list models as particularly dangerous or note higher injury risk relative to other cars satisfy the need.

narrative: The user seeks information on incidents where non‑combatants were killed in African armed conflicts, including casualty figures, dates, locations, and the parties involved; relevant documents should provide specific event descriptions, official or NGO reports, or aggregated statistics on civilian fatalities caused by combat.

narrative: The user is looking for information about recently approved, planned, or under‑construction hydroelectric projects, including details such as location, installed capacity, and construction status. narrative: The user wants comprehensive information on the health risks and adverse outcomes of dental implants, including infection, loosening, pain, foreign-body reactions, and other complications, as well as any regulatory or classification information that addresses safety. Relevant documents should discuss epidemiological studies, review reports, risk assessments, and any consensus statements that evaluate the strength of the association, including any indications of increased risk, uncertainty, or inconclusive findings.

title: US Ethnic Demographics
description: What are the sizes and percentages of the different ethnic groups in the United States population?
narrative: The user seeks quantitative data on the ethnic composition of the U.S. population, including counts or proportionate percentages for major groups such as White, Black/African American, Hispanic/Latino, Asian, Native American, and Pacific Islander. Relevant documents should present these statistics from reputable sources (e.g., U.S. Census Bureau, Population Reference Bureau) and indicate the year or time period of the data. Documents that only mention ethnicity incidentally (e.g., in a discussion of poverty) are not sufficient unless they provide explicit demographic figures.

title: OIC Balkans
description: What was the role and activities of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation in the Balkan region during the 1990s?
narrative: The user is looking for any information about the OIC's involvement in the Balkans in the 1990s, such as official statements, condemnations, resolutions, humanitarian aid, diplomatic initiatives, or meetings concerning Bosnia‑Herzegovina, Kosovo, Albania, or other Balkan territories (including islands). Relevant documents must mention the Organization of Islamic Cooperation by name and describe its actions or positions related to the Balkan conflicts of that decade.

title: ETA Basque
description: What is the ETA and what recent terrorist-related activities, arrests, or legal proceedings involving the Basque separatist organization have occurred?
narrative: The user wants a concise overview of the ETA as a Basque terrorist organization, including its designation as a terrorist group and any recent news about its members—such as arrests, trials, or asylum issues. Documents that mention ETA arrests, court cases, or its status as a terrorist organization are relevant, while unrelated terrorism reports are not.

title: ADD Diagnosis Treatment
description: What are the recommended methods for diagnosing Attention Deficit Disorder and the available treatment options?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive information on how Attention Deficit Disorder (ADD) is identified and managed. Relevant material should describe diagnostic criteria, assessment tools, and professional evaluation procedures, as well as outline effective treatments such as medication, behavioral therapy, and other interventions. Sources that merely mention ADD in passing or focus solely on unrelated learning disabilities without providing concrete diagnostic or therapeutic guidance are not considered relevant.

title: Child Lead Exposure
description: What are the health risks, primary sources (e.g., paint, water pipes, dust, soil), and protective regulations related to lead poisoning in children?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on lead poisoning in children, emphasizing how exposure occurs through lead‑based paint, contaminated water from lead pipes, dust, and soil, the resulting health effects on young children, and the child‑protection policies and regulations (such as EPA standards and the Residential Lead‑Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act) designed to prevent or mitigate these hazards. Relevant documents should discuss the health impact on children, identify major exposure pathways, and describe regulatory or remediation measures aimed at safeguarding children.

title: School Prayer Ban
description: What is the legal status and history of bans on school prayer in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks information about whether school prayer is prohibited in U.S. public schools, including the constitutional basis, Supreme Court rulings, legislative attempts, and political debates surrounding voluntary or mandated prayer. Relevant documents should discuss court decisions (e.g., Engel v. Vitale, Wallace v. Jaffree), federal and state policies, proposed constitutional amendments, and statements from policymakers about re‑introducing or maintaining bans on school prayer.

title: Teen Pregnancy Causes
description: What are the primary causes and risk factors contributing to teenage pregnancy among mothers in the United States?
narrative: The user is looking for a comprehensive overview of the factors that lead to teenage pregnancies in the U.S., including socioeconomic, educational, cultural, and health‑service influences, as well as any relevant statistical data on teenage mothers. Relevant documents should discuss the underlying causes or risk factors (e.g., poverty, lack of sex education, family background, access to contraception) or describe programs and policies that aim to address these causes (such as family planning counseling, preventive education, or community outreach). Documents that only list services without connecting them to the causes of teenage pregnancy are not considered relevant.

title: Cruise Safety Regulations
description: What health, safety, and maintenance standards must cruise ships meet?
narrative: The user is seeking authoritative information on the regulations, certifications, and operational requirements that govern health, safety, and maintenance on cruise vessels. Relevant documents should describe licensing or certificate requirements, passenger capacity limits, required lifesaving and fire‑fighting equipment, routine safety inspections, and any health‑related protocols that cruise operators must follow.

title: California Whale Watching
description: What are the best places and times to go whale watching along the California coast?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive information on whale‑watching opportunities in California, including which coastal locations (e.g., Channel Islands, Monterey Bay, Santa Barbara, San Diego) offer the highest likelihood of sightings, what species (gray, humpback, blue, etc.) can be seen, and the seasonal windows when migrations occur. Relevant documents should describe whale distribution, migration timing, and viewing hotspots along the California shoreline, and may also mention tour operators or observation points.

title: Melanoma Treatment Effects
description: What causes melanoma and what side effects or recurrence factors are associated with its treatments?
narrative: The user is looking for information on the etiological causes of melanoma as well as the adverse outcomes, resistance mechanisms, and diagnostic implications that arise from its treatment, particularly radiation‑based and particle‑beam therapies. Relevant material should discuss risk factors for melanoma, describe treatment modalities such as proton‑beam and neutron therapy, and explain how these treatments can lead to side effects, radiation resistance, or disease recurrence that affect diagnosis and patient management.

title: Telemarketing Consumer Protection
description: What legislation, regulations, and consumer‑protection strategies exist to shield people from unwanted or abusive telemarketing calls?
narrative: The user is seeking authoritative information on legal and regulatory frameworks that govern telemarketing practices, including statutes, industry rules, and government directives aimed at protecting consumers. Relevant documents should discuss specific laws (e.g., Do‑Not‑Call registries, data‑protection directives), enforcement agencies, compliance requirements for telemarketers, and practical strategies consumers can use to avoid or report intrusive calls. Materials that only describe telemarketing benefits or technology without addressing consumer‑protection measures are not relevant.

title: Agent Orange Health
description: What health problems are associated with exposure to Agent Orange among Vietnam veterans?
narrative: The user is looking for information on the medical effects of Agent Orange exposure, especially as they pertain to Vietnam veterans. Relevant material should describe the herbicide’s dioxin contamination, summarize epidemiological studies or official reports linking Agent Orange to specific illnesses (e.g., cancers, birth defects, neurological disorders, and other chronic conditions), and discuss any veteran health assessments, compensation programs, or policy findings related to these health outcomes.

title: American Indian Museum
description: What are the key details about the National Museum of the American Indian, such as its location, affiliation, collections, and visitor information?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive information on the National Museum of the American Indian, including its relationship to the Smithsonian Institution, its mission, exhibit highlights, address, opening hours, admission policies, and any relevant programs or staffing details. Relevant results should explicitly mention the museum, describe its purpose or services to the public, and provide practical visiting information.

title: Sub‑Saharan Poverty
description: What are the current levels, trends, and drivers of poverty in sub‑Saharan Africa?
narrative: The user wants up‑to‑date, region‑specific information on poverty in sub‑Saharan African countries, including prevalence rates, demographic breakdowns, historical trends, and the main causes such as conflict, economic policy, or environmental factors. Relevant documents should provide statistical data, analysis of underlying drivers, and discussion of major programs or policies aimed at reducing poverty in this region. Materials that only mention Africa generally, focus on unrelated political events, or lack substantive poverty content are not considered relevant.

title: Thatcher Resignation Impact
description: How did Margaret Thatcher’s resignation affect the political and diplomatic relationship between the United Kingdom and the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking information that explains the consequences of Thatcher’s departure for Anglo‑American relations, including shifts in diplomatic attitudes, policy decisions, and public perception in both countries. Relevant material should discuss the perceived loss of a strategic asset, the deterioration of ties in the early 1990s, specific incidents (e.g., visa controversies, arms sales, NATO/European leadership dynamics), and commentary on how the change in leadership altered U.S. and U.K. political interactions.

title: Unmarried Partner Households
description: What are the prevalence, characteristics, and trends of unmarried partner households in the United States?
narrative: The user seeks demographic and statistical information on U.S. households made up of unmarried partners, including how many exist, their share of total households, age, income, and regional distribution, as well as any recent trends or comparisons to married households. Relevant sources should provide census, survey, or research data that define and quantify these households.

title: Poverty Disease Link
description: What are the connections between poverty and disease?
narrative: The user wants information on how poverty influences the occurrence, spread, and impact of diseases. Relevant documents should describe epidemiological and socioeconomic mechanisms linking low income or deprivation to higher disease risk, discuss specific examples or studies of this relationship, and may include related factors such as environmental conditions, healthcare access, and public health outcomes.

title: Islamic Revolution Causes
description: What were the underlying reasons and motivations for the Islamic Revolution?
narrative: The user is looking for information that explains why the Islamic Revolution occurred, focusing on the political, social, economic, and ideological factors that motivated the movement. Relevant documents should discuss the grievances against the previous regime, opposition to foreign (especially U.S.) influence, religious motivations, economic hardships, and any statements by officials or scholars that outline the rationale behind the revolution. Materials that merely mention the revolution without addressing its causes are not sufficient.

title: US Election Apathy
description: What are the causes, trends, and statistics of low voter turnout and apathy in United States elections?
narrative: The user wants comprehensive information on voter apathy in the United States, including numerical turnout data, historical or recent trends, and explanations for why eligible voters abstain. Relevant documents should discuss low turnout percentages, analyses of underlying reasons (e.g., disengagement, demographic factors, candidate perception), and illustrative examples from federal, state, or local U.S. elections that shed light on the phenomenon.

title: Salvation Army Benefits
description: What types of assistance and benefits does the Salvation Army offer to people in need?
narrative: The user wants detailed information about the programs and services the Salvation Army provides, such as homeless shelter, food aid, financial assistance, disaster relief, counseling, and other forms of help, including eligibility criteria and how to access them. Relevant documents should describe the scope of these benefits and the mechanisms for obtaining support.

title: Soviet Afghanistan Withdrawal
description: What were the dates, reasons, and process of the Soviet Union's withdrawal of troops from Afghanistan?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of the Soviet withdrawal from Afghanistan, covering the political decision‑making (e.g., Gorbachev’s agreement under US pressure), the timeline and stages of troop removal, the number of forces withdrawn, any diplomatic agreements involved, and the immediate effects on the conflict and Cold‑War dynamics. Relevant material must directly address these aspects of the withdrawal; broader discussions of Soviet foreign policy that do not mention the Afghanistan pull‑out are not relevant.

title: Greenpeace Arrests
description: What arrests or prosecutions of Greenpeace activists have taken place?
narrative: The user seeks documented instances where Greenpeace members or the organization were legally detained, arrested, or prosecuted, including details such as date, location, cause (e.g., anti‑nuclear protests), and outcomes; relevant results should describe specific events like the 1992 French navy arrest at Mururoa and any other comparable legal actions.

title: Olympic Swimming Training
description: What training methods, camps, and preparation strategies do elite swimmers use to get ready for the Olympic Games?
narrative: The user is seeking detailed information on how Olympic swimmers train, including descriptions of training programs, conditioning routines, coaching techniques, and locations of training camps. Relevant material should discuss specific preparation practices, periodization, and athlete experiences related to Olympic swimming preparation, rather than merely reporting competition results.

title: Poppy Cultivation Export
description: How is poppy cultivated and exported, and what regulations and impacts are associated with it?
narrative: The user wants detailed information on poppy farming practices, the scale of production, export channels, and the legal or illicit trade environment, especially in regions such as Vietnam; relevant documents should cover cultivation methods, economic importance, export policies, and social consequences such as addiction.

title: Pisa Tower Status
description: What is the current condition and tilt of the Leaning Tower of Pisa?
narrative: The user is looking for up‑to‑date information on the Leaning Tower of Pisa, specifically recent measurements of its lean, any changes in its angle or position, and the overall structural status (e.g., safety, ongoing restoration, or stabilization efforts). Relevant documents should provide recent (post‑1990) data on the tower’s tilt, reports of movement, engineering assessments, or news about preservation work that indicates its present condition.

title: Joint Custody Impact
description: What are the pros and cons of shared or joint custody for children after a divorce?
narrative: The user wants information that evaluates how joint/shared custody arrangements affect children, covering psychological, emotional, developmental, legal, and financial aspects. Relevant material should discuss both advantages (e.g., maintaining relationships with both parents, stability, balanced involvement) and disadvantages (e.g., parental conflict, logistical challenges, potential stress) and may include research findings, expert commentary, case studies, or policy analysis. Documents that address systemic factors such as gender bias in family courts, cost implications, and the broader impact of custody decisions on child wellbeing are also considered relevant.

title: Adoption Record Access
description: What are the legal options for opening sealed adoption records in the United States and what is the current debate surrounding those policies?
narrative: The user is seeking information on how sealed adoption records can be accessed—such as court orders, state statutes, or legislative proposals—and wants to understand the ongoing public and legal debate about privacy versus adoptees' right to know. Relevant documents should discuss sealed adoption files, court rulings, state-by-state differences, advocacy positions, and any recent policy discussions or reforms related to opening these records.

title: Spanish Immersion Impact
description: What is the relationship between U.S. K‑12 Spanish immersion programs and the educational outcomes of Mexican immigrant students, and how might these programs influence immigration patterns?
narrative: The user seeks information on how Spanish language instruction in U.S. primary and secondary schools serves immigrant populations, particularly Mexican immigrants, and whether such programs are viewed as a factor in improving integration or affecting immigration flows. Relevant documents should discuss bilingual or immersion curricula, enrollment of immigrant children, academic performance, policy debates linking language education to immigration, and any evidence of program impacts on Mexican immigration trends.

title: Wind Power Sites
description: What locations are suitable or currently used for wind power generation?
narrative: The user is seeking information about geographic sites where wind power installations exist or could be developed, including details such as specific regions, terrain, wind resource quality, and examples of existing wind farms. Relevant documents should describe the location (e.g., country, region, altitude, nearby landmarks) of wind power projects and explain why those sites are favorable for wind energy exploitation.

title: Adult Immigrant English
description: What English language education programs and resources are available for adult immigrants in the United States?
narrative: The user is seeking information about formal and informal English education initiatives targeting adult immigrants, including federal or state programs, grant opportunities, eligibility criteria, services offered (e.g., literacy, ESL classes, workplace English), and any associated funding or partnership details. Relevant documents should describe such programs, their objectives, target populations, and implementation mechanisms, such as the National Workplace Literacy Program and related regulations.

title: Czechoslovakia Breakup
description: What were the events, agreements, and property‑division processes that led to the dissolution of Czechoslovakia into the Czech Republic and Slovakia?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive overview of the 1992‑1993 breakup of Czechoslovakia, including the political decision to split, the legal framework for the separation, and the detailed economic settlements such as the division of federal property, banks, gold, budget deficits, archives, airlines, maritime fleet, and other assets. Relevant material should describe the timeline of negotiations, the agreements that have been signed, those still pending, and the specific issues each successor state faced during the division of the former federation’s assets.

title: Part-time Benefits
description: What benefits, especially medical/health insurance, are available to part‑time employees?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on the benefit packages that part‑time workers may receive. Relevant material should describe the types of benefits (health/medical insurance, dental, vision, paid time off, retirement plans, disability, etc.), the eligibility criteria (hours worked per week, employer size, state or federal regulations such as the ACA, FMLA, COBRA, or state mandates), and any differences between full‑time and part‑time offerings. Sources that discuss employer policies, legal requirements, recent legislative changes, or comparative analyses of part‑time benefit coverage are considered relevant.

title: Oscar Selection Process
description: How are Oscar winners selected?
narrative: The user wants a clear explanation of the procedures used to determine Oscar winners, including who votes, how Academy members are chosen, the voting mechanics, eligibility rules for films and nominees, and any official guidelines or stages of the selection process. Relevant documents should describe the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences, its voting membership, and the methods by which votes are cast and counted to award the Oscars.

title: Argentina Dollar Peg
description: What is the history, structure, and economic impact of Argentina's pegging of the peso to the US dollar?
narrative: The user seeks comprehensive information on Argentina's currency peg to the US dollar, including when and how the peg was implemented, its legal or policy mechanisms, and the resulting economic problems such as inflation, loss of competitiveness, trade effects, and any subsequent reforms. Relevant documents should discuss the dollar‑peso exchange rate policy, its advantages and drawbacks, and contextual analysis of Argentina’s monetary challenges.

title: Northern Ireland Industry
description: Identify the major industries and leading companies operating in Northern Ireland.
narrative: The user wants an overview of Northern Ireland's business landscape, including the principal economic sectors (such as manufacturing, food processing, engineering, chemicals, construction, etc.) and representative firms within each sector. Relevant documents should mention companies located in Northern Ireland, describe their products or services, and clearly indicate the industry they belong to, thereby illustrating the region's industrial composition.

title: European Media Bias
description: What are the characteristics, causes, and examples of media bias in non‑U.S. outlets, especially across Europe?
narrative: The user is seeking information that explains how media bias manifests outside the United States, with a focus on European news organizations. Relevant material should discuss the types of bias (political, cultural, ownership‑driven, etc.), provide comparative analyses or case studies of European outlets, cite scholarly research, watchdog reports, or reputable surveys, and distinguish these biases from those in U.S. media. Documents that merely mention U.S. media or unrelated topics are not relevant.

title: US Family Planning Aid
description: What U.S. foreign assistance programs support family planning, contraception, education, and clinic services abroad?
narrative: The user wants information on United States foreign aid initiatives that fund or deliver family planning services overseas, including contraception distribution, educational outreach, and clinic support. Relevant material should describe U.S. agencies (such as USAID, CDC, State Department), program goals, funding levels, target countries or regions, and outcomes related to family planning assistance.

title: College ROI
description: How does obtaining a college degree affect employment prospects and earnings in the United States?
narrative: The user is looking for evidence that a college education provides a measurable advantage in the US labor market. Relevant material should discuss job placement rates, salary differentials, or career advancement associated with a bachelor’s degree versus lower levels of education, include statistical data, trends over time, or comparisons across industries, and ideally cite studies, government reports, or reputable surveys that quantify the impact of college attainment on job opportunities.

title: Clear‑cutting Protests
description: What are the current practices, regulations, and public protests related to clear‑cutting of forests?
narrative: The user seeks information that explains how clear‑cutting is performed (methods, typical tract sizes, ownership differences), what regulations or policy limits exist, and any documented opposition, protests, or controversy surrounding these practices. Relevant documents should describe clear‑cutting operations, governmental or industry guidelines, and mention protests, activist actions, or public debate about the environmental impact of clear‑cutting.

title: Prostate Cancer Care
description: What are the current methods for detecting prostate cancer and the available treatment options?
narrative: The user seeks a comprehensive overview of prostate cancer management, including screening and diagnostic techniques (e.g., PSA testing, digital rectal exam, imaging, biopsy) as well as therapeutic approaches (surgery, radiation, hormone therapy, chemotherapy, newer pharmacological agents, and clinical trial options). Relevant documents should describe detection procedures, diagnostic accuracy, guidelines, and detail various treatment modalities, their indications, effectiveness, and side‑effect profiles.

title: Media Convergence Effects
description: How does electronic media influence newspapers in terms of readership, content, ownership, and overall viability?
narrative: The user seeks information on the impact of electronic media (e.g., television, satellite TV, digital platforms) on the newspaper industry. Relevant documents should discuss changes such as newspapers shifting toward TV, cross‑media ownership, declines in circulation, alterations in editorial content, financial challenges, and broader social implications of this convergence.

title: Compost Pile Guide
description: How can I set up and maintain a compost pile using kitchen scraps, vegetable waste, and worms, including how and when to turn it?
narrative: The user is looking for practical instructions on creating a home compost pile: selecting a location, building the pile or bin, what types of waste (food scraps, vegetable residues, other organic material) are acceptable, how to introduce and care for composting worms, and the correct frequency and technique for turning the pile to promote decomposition. Relevant information should cover step‑by‑step setup, material ratios, worm bin integration, turning schedules, and tips for successful composting.

title: White Collar Sentencing
description: What are the typical or average prison sentences imposed for white‑collar crimes?
narrative: The user is seeking information on how long individuals convicted of white‑collar offenses are generally sentenced, including any average sentence lengths, typical ranges, or illustrative examples from real cases. Relevant documents should contain sentencing details for white‑collar crimes—such as specific years served, averages, or comparative analysis—and may cite notable cases (e.g., Ernest Saunders, Gerald Ronson) that illustrate these sentencing outcomes.

title: Plastic Surgery Safety
description: What are the risks, safety records, and regulatory safeguards for cosmetic and plastic surgery procedures?
narrative: The user is seeking reliable information about the safety of plastic and cosmetic surgery, including common complications, statistical safety data, and any consumer‑protection regulations that govern how providers must represent risks. Relevant documents should discuss documented safety outcomes, required scientific evidence for safety claims, and legal actions (e.g., FTC orders) that address misrepresentation of safety, side‑effects, and risks.

title: ATC Working Conditions
description: What are the working conditions for air traffic controllers in the United States?
narrative: The user is looking for comprehensive information about the job environment of U.S. air traffic controllers, including typical shift schedules, stress levels, health and safety concerns, salary and benefits, union representation, and any FAA regulations that directly affect their working conditions. Relevant documents should describe the duties of controllers, the operational context of control facilities, and policies or reports that address workload, fatigue management, staffing levels, or workplace standards for controllers employed by the FAA or other U.S. aviation authorities.

title: African Literacy
description: What are the current literacy rates for African countries and regions, especially sub‑Saharan Africa?
narrative: The user seeks up‑to‑date statistical information on literacy levels across Africa, including adult and youth literacy percentages, regional comparisons (e.g., sub‑Saharan vs. North Africa), trends over time, and credible sources such as UNESCO or World Bank reports. Relevant documents should contain quantitative literacy data, discuss gaps between African regions and other parts of the world, and provide context on factors influencing those rates. Documents that only address unrelated aspects of education (e.g., expenditure per pupil, higher education enrollment) without presenting literacy statistics are not relevant.

title: Term Limits Debate
description: What are the pros and cons of imposing term limits on elected officials?
narrative: The user is seeking information that presents arguments both in favor of and against term limits for elected officials, including discussion of how limits might affect corruption, experience, power distribution, and democratic governance. Relevant documents should contain balanced analysis, examples, or commentary that outlines the benefits (e.g., reduced corruption, broader power sharing) and the drawbacks (e.g., loss of experience, bureaucratic continuity) of term‑limit policies.

title: US Gasoline Tax
description: What are the arguments for and against implementing or increasing a gasoline tax in the United States?
narrative: The user wants an overview of U.S. gasoline taxation, including current rates, policy proposals, and a balanced discussion of the pros and cons. Relevant material should cover economic, fiscal, and environmental arguments, the impact on the federal deficit, and viewpoints from business groups, policymakers, and analysts about whether a gasoline tax is beneficial or detrimental.
